behaviors.description: Required patient fields (name, date of birth, insurance ID) are validated before any downstream processing; missing fields return all missing items.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: validation result must both (1) aggregate missing fields and (2) prevent downstream processing. Hypothetical elsewhere: payer API/network or AI triage quality. Chosen level is Integration because the real failure boundary is validator-to-orchestrator handoff (downstream must not run). Rejection gate: System/Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because behavior text contains no real OS/network/subprocess requirement and no dependence on AI output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run intake orchestration with an input missing multiple required fields while wiring real validator + orchestrator + mocked downstream services that record calls; assert error lists all missing fields and zero downstream modules are invoked.

behaviors.description: Date of birth validation rejects future dates, dates older than 150 years, and non-date strings.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: pure date parsing/range logic. Hypothetical elsewhere: orchestration, DB, network, AI. Chosen level is Unit because failure is within one validation function/object boundary with no module handoff required. Rejection gate: Integration+ disqualified by behavior text being strictly input-validation rules.
behaviors.test_description: Table-driven tests against DOB validator for valid DOB, future DOB, DOB older than 150 years, and malformed strings; assert specific rejection outcomes.

behaviors.description: Insurance eligibility check sends correct patient ID and plan code to payer API and interprets eligible/not eligible/pending responses.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: correctness of outbound API payload and interpretation of real API response states. Hypothetical elsewhere: scheduler race conditions, confirmation formatting. Chosen level is System because real HTTP contract/serialization failures can be hidden by in-process mocks (MOCK-HIDE). Rejection gate: Integration was considered but rejected since behavior explicitly verifies what is sent to the payer API, which is an external HTTP boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Execute eligibility client against a real HTTP test server endpoint (no mocked transport), assert received request contains correct patient ID/plan code, and verify mapping for eligible/not eligible/pending response bodies/statuses.

behaviors.description: If payer API is unreachable, eligibility is marked pending and intake continues instead of blocking.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: handling real API unreachability and continuation of processing. Hypothetical elsewhere: AI triage quality. Chosen level is System because failure mode is infrastructure/network reachability; pure mocks can mask real timeout/connection behavior. Rejection gate: Integration rejected because behavior text explicitly centers on API being unreachable (real infra failure class).
behaviors.test_description: Run intake with eligibility call pointed to a non-routable/closed endpoint or forced connection timeout in real HTTP client; assert eligibility becomes pending and pipeline proceeds to later steps.

behaviors.description: AI symptom triage assigns high urgency for emergency symptom combinations (e.g., chest pain + shortness of breath).
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: correctness depends on medical urgency judgment produced by AI model output. Hypothetical elsewhere: API retry wiring or DB transaction details. Chosen level is Agentic because deterministic stubs cannot detect model-quality failures (LLM-DEP yes). Rejection gate: Integration rejected since behavior asks for correctness of triage decision itself, not routing to model.
behaviors.test_description: Send emergency symptom cases to the real triage model repeatedly and assert high-urgency classification meets acceptance threshold across runs; include non-emergency controls to detect over-escalation.

behaviors.description: Symptom triage returns a clinician-reviewable natural language explanation, not only a numeric score.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: generated explanation quality/format must be natural language and clinically reviewable. Hypothetical elsewhere: scheduler slot search. Chosen level is Agentic because output quality depends on real model generation; stubs would always satisfy format checks and hide failures. Rejection gate: Integration rejected because behavior evaluates generated content, not just schema plumbing.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke real triage model with representative symptom inputs; assert response includes explanatory prose meeting defined readability/completeness checks and is not score-only output.

behaviors.description: Scheduling picks first available slot matching urgency SLA: high same-day, medium within 48 hours.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: scheduler logic + calendar data interaction to enforce urgency windows. Hypothetical elsewhere: external payer uptime or AI model variability. Chosen level is Integration because failure occurs at module/data boundary (scheduler + shared calendar DB), with no required real external network/AI dependency. Integration-brake check: no real OS/network/subprocess required; no model-output dependency; no multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Seed local calendar DB with controlled availability; run scheduling service for high/medium urgency and assert chosen slot is earliest valid and within required time window.

behaviors.description: Scheduling never double-books a slot when two patients are processed concurrently.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: concurrent writes against shared calendar database and booking uniqueness. Hypothetical elsewhere: payer API semantics, LLM explanation quality. Chosen level is Integration because the real boundary is app concurrency + DB constraints/transactions in local environment. Integration-brake check: no external network or AI correctness dependency; concurrency can be reproduced with in-process parallel workers against local DB. Rejection gate: System considered, but behavior text does not require subprocess/OS-specific primitives beyond local concurrent module interaction.
behaviors.test_description: Run two concurrent scheduling attempts targeting same open slot against local calendar DB with real transaction/locking configuration; assert exactly one booking succeeds and no duplicate row exists.

behaviors.description: Confirmation message includes patient name, appointment time, provider name, and visit-type-specific preparation instructions.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: message composition/content requirements. Hypothetical elsewhere: transport channel delivery failures, external APIs. Chosen level is Unit because failure is template/composition logic in one component; no required multi-module or infra boundary. Rejection gate: Integration+ disqualified because behavior text checks message content, not cross-service delivery.
behaviors.test_description: Test confirmation formatter with fixture inputs for different visit types; assert rendered message contains required fields and correct preparation instructions.

behaviors.description: End-to-end intake pipeline (validate, insurance, triage, schedule, confirm) completes automatically for a real patient submission.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: full pipeline execution including AI triage plus non-AI steps with no manual intervention. Hypothetical elsewhere: isolated unit-level parsing edge cases. Chosen level is Workflow because this is explicitly a full pipeline and includes an agentic step in sequence with other stages (MULTI-STEP full flow). Rejection gate: Agentic/System alone rejected since behavior text requires complete cross-step pipeline completion, not just one model call or one infrastructure boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Execute a production-like intake run with real submission through all stages using real triage model and live-integrated components; assert terminal success state, persisted appointment, and emitted confirmation without human override.
